sglang.title: [Fix & Style] Refactor the grammar backend to reduce human errors and improve readability
sglang.author: DarkSharpness
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Recently, several state-of-the-art constrained-decoding features have been integrated into sglang, such as llguidance (#3298) and structural tag (#3566). However, the legacy grammar backends lack proper support for these new features, which can potentially cause server crashes.
For examp

sglang.title: [XCCL] Use xccl for xpu backend since xccl is ready in latest PyTorch.
sglang.author: cboss6
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Use xccl for xpu backend since xccl is ready in latest PyTorch(pytorch/pytorch#135791).
I have verified in my local environment with sglang-0.4.3.post2 and pytorch-2.7.0a0+git4bacd13 based on Intel XPU backend:
python3 -m sglang.bench_one_batch --batch-size 1 --input 32 --output 32 --tp-

sglang.title: fix: support gelu_new activation function in gpt2
sglang.author: Xiuyu-Li
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

GPT-2 models use the gelu_new activation function, which sglang currently does not support, causing get_act_fn to throw an error. Previously, this was handled by the NewGELU vllm dependency. This PR adds a native sglang NewGELU CustomOp with forward_native support, following huggingface/

sglang.title: remove unused max_jobs
sglang.author: sgjzfzzf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The max_jobs is controlled by the environment variable, not the parameter, so it's invalid, in fact. Please refer to the BuildExtension source code in the PyTorch repo.

Modifications
Remove invalid parameters in the setup script.

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Format

vllm.title: [platform] add debug logging during inferring the device type
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
there are quite a lot reports of failed to infer device type , and right now it's difficult to tell what's happening.
this pr adds the logging when we infer the device type, then users can set VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=DEBUG to figure out what's happening.

vllm.title: [sleep mode] error out with expandable_segments
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
as reported in #11743 (comment) , and confirmed with the pytorch team at pytorch/pytorch#147851 (comment) , the sleep mode implementation is not compatible with expandable_segments, and it cannot be fixed in the short term.
let's give users clear error message until that is solved from pytorch side.

vllm.title: [doc] add "Failed to infer device type" to faq
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: followup of #14195

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Restrict MacOS CPU detection
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: There is a concern that the ARM CPU detection would affect NVIDIA ARM devices like GH200, so we should restrict the special case to just MacOS as it was intended for in #12227

vllm.title: [V1][BugFix] Fix remaining sync engine client shutdown errors/hangs
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
These seem to stem from race conditions with the output processor thread while closing the zmq sockets.
Use an inproc socket to signal shutdown to the output socket processing thread.

vllm.title: [V0][Metrics] Deprecate some questionable request time metrics
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
vllm:time_in_queue_requests appears to be an exact duplicate of vllm:request_queue_time_seconds.
Both record first_scheduled_time-arrival_time:
if seq_group.is_finished():
    time_queue_requests.append(
        seq_group.metrics.first_scheduled_time -
        seq_group.metrics.arrival_time)

def ma

vllm.author: lk-chen

Expected: get_multimodal_embeddings() should return list[Tensor] for GPUModelRunner to iterate.
Actual: prior to this PR, molmo's _get_mm_embeds() returns a list thus get_multimodal_embeddings() returns a list of list.
The issue is surfaced when all of following satisfy:

more than one request
the t

vllm.title: add cutlass support for blackwell fp8 gemm
vllm.author: kushanam
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for cutlass blackwell gemm for fp8
A couple of notes:
1- The sm100_fp8_config_default is the only supported config for now with static tile and cluster shapes. Consequent PRs will support optimized configs for other shapes. Also 2xsm gemm will be added.
2- added default construc

vllm.title: [TPU][Profiler] Support start_profile/stop_profile in TPU worker
vllm.author: lsy323
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Implement start_profile and stop_profile method in TPU worker. This allows user to enable profiling in benchmark_serving.py, which calls start_profile and stop_profile to control the profiling session.

Update torchxla version to include the required profiler support in pytorch/xla#8743
Add start_pr

vllm.title: Fix performance when `--generation-config` is not `None`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adds self.default_sampling_params to:

OpenAIServingChat
OpenAIServingCompletion
OpenAIServingTranscription
LLM

As you can see from the benchmarks below, the performance difference is huge:
vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct --disable-log-requests --generation-config auto

python benchmark

vllm.title: [Frontend] Do `prompt_logprobs` clamping for chat as well as completions
vllm.author: hmellor

docker run \
   --rm \
   --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" \
   --workdir /workspace \

vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Related to #10582. Some notes I had taken on v0 metrics implementation, along with v1 design details.

vllm.title: Serialize using safetensors for KV caches
vllm.author: KuntaiDu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Serializing the KV caches using safetensors instead of pickle in Mooncake Pipe for safety.
Note that I just tested the behavior of this class locally, assuming that the received bytes and the sent bytes are the same. @ShangmingCai would be great if you can also check and see if it breaks Mooncake fu

vllm.title: Clean up unused padding_idx variables across many model definitions
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR cleans up a bunch of unused padding_idx variables across many model definitions.
It looks like these are intended to be passed to the VocabParallelEmbedding operation. Trying to get the full story of why these unused variables exist before landing this.

I'm somewhat concerned about the max diffs in the test_per_token_group_quant_fp8 and I'm open to removing the kernels/test_block_fp8.py test from t

vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Now that #13379 has landed, this is an updated version of #13496 😁
Currently only focused on usability and correctness for Llava-style multimodal models, not performance.
This does not deal with pre-compiling the encoder forward pass, so in the event that the model is passed in image/video/audio tha

vllm.title: Moved numba from common requirements to cuda/rocm specific requirements
vllm.author: npanpaliya
vllm.bodyExcerpt: As per this comment https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/13365/files#r1960689585, numba shouldn't be a part of requirements-common.txt. So, moved it to cuda/rocm specific requirements file. This will also help us not to numba in cpu only vllm.

DP+EP implemented via collective ops in the fused_moe layer's forward pass.

vllm.title: Temporarily disable test_awq_gemm_opcheck
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The failing kernel test of test_awq.py:test_awq_gemm_opcheck was clearly started by the GPTQAllSpark integration (#12931), however I have no clue while as it isn’t directly related to AWQ. We have been unable to reproduce the failure locally on H100 or A100, so we want to skip this test for now.

vllm.title: [Frontend] Allow return_tokens_as_token_ids to be passed as a request param
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
PR #6626 contributed a helpful change to allow the logprob token strings to instead denote the tokenized ids instead of the strings, which is useful for non-ascii streaming.
In some cases, a serving engine must service requests which want this feature enabled, and requests which want the string repr

vllm.title: [Misc][V1] Avoid using `envs.VLLM_USE_V1` in mm processing
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The main difference between V0 and V1 multimodal input processing is that in V1 we need mm_hashes for downstream tasks (prefix caching, feature caching). Since input processing has separate codepaths between V0 and V1,  we can simply remove the need to check V1 envvar for this logic.
